description:
src/git.rs:284 defines git_ensure_user() which sets a default user.email/user.name when the repo has none. It is only called from Store::init(). Every other command path (cmd_create, cmd_claim, cmd_review, cmd_close, cmd_sync, cmd_dep, cmd_link, cmd_drop, cmd_update) performs git operations without ensuring identity, so bl is unusable on any fresh system (and every test that runs bl in a scratch dir hangs CI forever — see bl-548e, bl-941f, bl-1e60, bl-2b7e).

Fix: call git_ensure_user() from Store::discover() so every command path auto-configures before any commit. Alternative: call it inside git_commit() in src/git.rs. Pick the cleanest spot — both achieve the same result.

Tests must add a fresh-install regression: spawn bl in a temp dir with an empty HOME and no global git config, run 'bl init && bl create foo && bl claim $(bl ready --json | ...) && bl review', all must succeed without hanging.

Do not set GIT_AUTHOR_* env in CI — that would mask the bug. The code must work on a system with no git identity.
title: Call git_ensure_user() on every command path
